preamble: A common historical shift where a voiced velar stop undergoes palatalization and subsequent affrication, particularly before front vowels or the palatal glide [j]. This shift, often occurring via a palatal stage [ɟ], is central to the Slavic First Palatalization and the development of the Romance languages (e.g., Latin 'gentem' > Italian 'gente' [dʒ]).

examples.note: Vocative form showing palatalization of /g/ before front vowel /e/.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
